backfill_note: The essay was written 2026-07-09 against lancedb 0.34.0; the original tmp harness (tmp/firsthand-lancedb-embedded-vector-lakehouse/) was discarded per the finite-disk policy. probe.py here is reconstructed from the recorded methodology in the workspace firsthand-benchmark.md so a third party can reproduce the method. The reconstructed harness was re-run on 2026-07-18 against the same pinned 0.34.0 — see reproduction below and results.json.reproduction_2026_07_18.

drift.exp_e_tuned_recall: 0.9 on re-run vs 1.0 in the authoring note — PQ codebook init not fully pinned; tuned recall lands in a 0.9–1.0 band.
drift.exp_f_bm25: 'fox' top-2 ids reproduce ([3,0]); absolute BM25 scores drift and the FTS corpus text was not recorded, so it is reconstructed, not bit-exact.

environment.note: Embedded library over local files only — no server, no network, no LLM. Hardware is irrelevant to the behavioral/recall results; timing was not measured.

subject.name: lancedb
subject.note: 0.34 deprecated create_fts_index and metric=/num_partitions= kwargs in favor of create_index('col', config=IvfPq(...)) / create_index(config=FTS()). The harness uses the 0.34 API.

baseline.name: exact brute-force search (numpy)
baseline.note: EXP E compares the IVF_PQ approximate index against an exact L2 brute-force ground truth computed in numpy. No external baseline tool executed.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: none large — evidence is small and committed
raw_artifacts.note: The 2026-07-09 tmp build dir (venv + on-disk .lance tables) was discarded post-publish per the disk policy. The committed evidence — probe.py (harness) + probe-result.json (deterministic re-run output) — is small and regenerable via run.sh.
